title: Coelogyne mindanaensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne mindanaensis is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as "hat orchids" or "dancing hats" due to their unique flower shape. The genus Coelogyne is part of the tribe Coelogyninae, which is a subtribe of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This subtribe is further divided into several genera, with Coelogyne being one of the largest and most diverse. Coelogyne mindanaensis is a species within this diverse genus.

intro: Coelogyne mindanaensis, also known as Mindanao Coelogyne, is an epiphytic orchid species native to the Philippines. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Coelogyne mindanaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
